sentences.text: Hey, good morning, Sam.
Hey, Austin, how's it going?
Not too bad, how's your week going?
Yeah, pretty good.
Got Thursday Night Football tonight, so super pumped.
Hell yeah, I'm, if you recall… we're up here… I'm in Wisconsin, so I'm.
Yeah, I figured I, you'd be a Packers fan.
I looked up, your LinkedIn, so… I'm a huge fan.
You're a Redskins fan?
Yeah, yeah.
Nice.
Nice.
Should be a good one.
Yeah, it should.
Jaden Daniels, and see how he actually can get around with Parsons now, so that'd be, like, a fun matchup to see him in.
Yeah, I was trying to, I was trying to fly out there, and it's a hard place to get to, though.
It's like a couple-hour drive from any airport, so I figured I'd bail on that one.
Yeah, this would be the time to go, but it is hard.
Like, you would have to fly into, like, Milwaukee or Madison, and both are, like, over an hour away.
Green Bay has, like, a super small airport, but it's probably super expensive.
I needed richer friends with private jets.
Justin, where are you from?
I've lived in Scottsdale, Arizona for the last 15 years, but I was born and raised in Pittsburgh, so I'm a Steeler fan.
Gotcha.
Not a big fan of that fan base, but that's alright.
AFC, so…
Yeah, for sure.
And are you… you're in Dallas now, or how…
I mean, I'm in the district, so I'm still, Northern Virginia, I guess, but, one of our satellite offices here, so we have offices in, Virginia, Boston, Houston, Dallas, Atlanta, and Florida.
Yeah, I didn't know you guys had one up in that area.
I knew, like, the Atlanta and Dallas office, but… nice.
Is it a smaller one, then?
Yeah, it's about…
15-ish people.
Okay.
Very consistently in here, yeah.
Okay, cool.
Yeah.
Well, appreciate you jumping on, and yeah, Justin's my counterpart here.
I think he was on a few of our other calls, I'm not sure.
He's been working with June for a long time now, so probably a couple years, so, figured to have him on here as well, if you haven't met.
But happy to go over, excited to show, kind of, that new Explore Premium.
And also just get kind of any feedback so far.
I know we're probably setting up a call with the larger group here in, like, a week or so, but…
Yup.
I know I've seen you've been in there quite a bit, so I'd love to hear kind of anything that you have so far, but…
Yeah, I mean, I can start off with kind of where I'm at in the testing and my thoughts, if you want to just do that, and then you can jump into the new stuff.
Yeah, that works for us.
Cool.

Yeah, so I mean, essentially the way I've been using it is, if I have a daily task or things that I would have otherwise used, CoStar, or used, RealPage Performance Analytics or any of our other kind of market research tools.
I've just been opting to use them, try it out in here.
Yeah.

You've probably seen, like, a lot of the reports that I've created were, you know, requests I've gotten from senior leadership, or from development, or from, operations, where I would use that other data, or just I was trying to answer a question, myself.
A good example is the most recent one, Gables Baldwin Park.
So I'm, going down there, that's in a development in Florida, so it's in Lisa.

in the Orlando area, and I'm going down at the end of the month to do an amenity review of the building, so I'm trying to familiarize myself with the market, understand what amenities are out there, and that one was focused more on, okay, like, let me see that amenity checklist of what different competitors have in the area, and what's kind of standard, so… I've really been using it for, a large I guess, wide range of, of reasons, and I've really liked it so far.
It's a new, easy-to-kind of digest, application that's, you know, when I show it to people, people ask a lot more questions, and that's been kind of, I guess, the problem that I've run into.
Which is a good problem in tech, right, is that you give people a cookie and they want more.
Right, yeah.

So, that's where this kind of came up, was, oh, hey, like, you know, I've been sharing these reports, or referencing some of these reports, and the question was, well, hey, you know, in CoStar, we used to get these full-on, broader market reports.

With kind of these customizable date ranges of, you know, year-over-year comparisons, and hey, I want to take these 3 months this year and compare it to those 3 months last year, and and I think that's where you guys can probably separate yourself from some of the competition, given the history of data that you guys have compared to others.
But yeah, I didn't know where to start, so that's where I reached out.
Okay.

Yeah, well, appreciate that feedback, and yeah, I had heard that a lot, like, once you start sharing reports, people have a lot of questions to follow, like, hey, where, you know, like, where's this data come from?
How can I get more?
So, totally understand that.
as far as, kind of, we can show that, like, year over year, quarter over quarter, and exactly right, like, we've had this data now 5 years, so October 2020 is where it goes back to.
So, I have a ton of that data, and we actually, about a quarter ago, hired one of the CoStar's top forecasters, so now we also have forecasting.
with that data and our new supply, so something we're also excited about.
But, sounds like market comparisons as well as, like, specific sub-markets.
Also, you said, like, even, like, downtown, like, areas, is that basically what you're trying to compare?
Yep, exactly.

So, you know, looking at downtown Austin, saying this is the way the market trended, this was where our internal property trended, and are we hitting that mark or not hitting that mark is, I think, a pretty valuable tool, for us to be able to understand.
Okay, yeah.
Well, we have that now live, so happy to kind of share that.
Anything else before we dive into that?

Anything, like, with… so, like, you're pulling those research reports, and on the market survey side, anything else… so you're leveraging, like, the amenities, obviously, like, the performance metrics, anything else before we get into this on that side?

Well, one thing that I did see, I was, you know, adding and removing comps, and for whatever reason, I want to say when I added comps, I didn't quite see it, like, it didn't show up, so let's say, like, I had 7 comps that were automatically added for a portfolio, for a property, and I added, like, an eighth one.
It didn't really show up.
until I exported the report, so I wasn't sure if it, like, added through or didn't add through, but when I exported the report, it did… did go through.
Okay.
That's something you've seen before.
Yeah, I have.
The quick way around it is just, when you add and remove, I always just do a quick refresh, and then it adds it in there.
And it'll be on there, okay.

Yeah, so when you're just, like, coming in here, like, if, yeah, say we wanted to add place at 5th right now, and, like, you just came back, place at 5th, not quite in there, but then I just hit the refresh, and then place at 5th would be right in there.
Right there.
Gotcha.
Okay.
So yeah, and then that makes sense.
It will, like, obviously, it will respect it then when you export it, but for you to, like, see it live in an app, just give it a refresh.
Yeah.
And then, the other just quick question,

You know, one of the things that I was kind of using the data for was leveraging the, capture of market share that I'm seeing, so… you know, if I'm looking at a group of competitors, and I'm seeing that over the last 30 days in one bedrooms, this competitor got a larger percentage of the market share.
And then referencing that back to where they're rent positioned across that subset.

are those things that you could build out within the application, or like… like, if I wanted to say, hey, you know, this guy was indexed at the highest rent, but also indexed at the highest capture of market share, what is going on at that building?
Because I want whatever's in their sauce.
Right.

So, like, the ability to, I guess, add other metrics within the system and within the reports, is that something that's possible down the line, or is that more of a, hey, you know, we're just giving you the data, you guys can put it in your BI tools, or however you want to kind of manipulate it from there?
Yeah, you're…
Go ahead, Justin.
Yeah, I was gonna say, Sam, so right now, the way it sits, you have to throw it into your BI tool.
What's nice is, on this immediate roadmap, we have, like, a report builder, so pretty much you'll be able to drop whatever metrics you want on your X and Y axis and build your own reports.
Okay.
So, I don't know if you've ever used, like, Tableau before.
Yeah.
fairly similar to that.

So yeah, I mean, there's so many cool conclusions that you can draw, so I think that's the idea behind it, is just, you know, taking all these data points and letting you figure out, you know, what makes sense, or let you undercover why a property might be performing, or a floor plan might be performing the way it is.
Yeah, gotcha.

It would just be cool, it's, it would be easy if, or when you guys, you know, have that within the system to say, hey, like, I'm gonna put these in, and I want these specific metrics, that way a less sophisticated user can just, you know, still export the same report and doesn't have to do anything within Tableau.
For sure.
Yeah, and it's a, I think it's, like, a fairly imminent release.
We have, like, our product roadmap, and it's, like, in the up-next stage, so, they never, they never give us specific dates, because, like, the salespeople will tell you it's coming, you know.
We switched over to our new revenue management software this year, and I just hear the word roadmap, and I'm like, yeah, but what does that actually mean?
I know, yeah.
Some kind of timeline.
Right.
Yeah, I mean, it's a top priority for us.
It's in that next… it's in that up next bucket, and I always hate saying that, but that's where it is.
Cool.

Yeah, I was gonna say, too, in, like, the meantime, I know a lot of folks within the app, if you looked at this historical section, you can scroll down to leased units, and you can look at it month over month, or, you know, week over week, and this will show you leased units by property, month over month, or week over week.
Yeah.
Yeah, you know, my math was just saying, okay, that capture the market share, and then where their rent was at, so it's kind of, you know, that two…
Yeah, yeah, you definitely would have to piece it together still, because then the rent's up here, so totally understand what you're saying.
Yeah, I know, just trying to…
Some data to be able to come to that conclusion.
I really like that.
Because then I can tell the, the on-site teams that it's not our price, it's.
Right.
Exactly.
Well, cool.
Other than that, I can get into the Explorer Premium, then.

So, basically, if you've poked around at all in the Explore area that you have currently, the update will be, functionally the same thing, so I can type in, you know, Dallas MSA here, and then you'll notice this new rent and performance and year-over-year growth section.
So, exactly that question that you asked.
I mean, you can see, year… year over year, quarter over quarter, kind of however you want, month over month, you can select whatever kind of time frame you want to look.
Also, not only rent rate, but we also have net effective rent in here, too.
So, this will give you that at the last 36 months or 3 years.
Any questions on that?
And then we also have, then, the forecasting piece that is coming in.
No, that looks great.
Okay, and then I was gonna say, too, the other cool mark, like, this is specific then for, like, this specific market that you're looking at, but then we also have added to this market comparison.
So this is where then you could, those specific markets, so I'll, like, I'll add a filter, like you said, you know, like, Austin.
And then Dallas.
You could just put those two in there, apply filter, and then you just have a clean view on exactly how those two have been performing.
And then, as you scroll over, we have, like, the year-over-year rent growth, you know, trailing 90 days, all those metrics.
you can also apply.
So, like, I have them all selected right now, but, like, you could make this very condensed and clean to what specifically you want to see.
Huh.
Can you go back to the main, the Market Insights tab real quick?
Yep.
Can you quickly just explain, and I may just be completely overlooking something, the quarterly drop-down next to the rent there?
So when you're looking at quarterly, monthly, yearly, how does that change?
Is that just the view of the graph?
Yeah, it's gonna… Exactly, yeah.
Okay, and that's…
It's all the same money.
any of the values.
No, it's the same… it's all the same data, it's just bigger, bigger, like, boxes, I guess you would say.
Okay.

I could have sworn when I was playing around with it the other day, when I changed it to monthly, quarterly, that was my expectation, that it was just gonna change the graph, and I thought I saw values change as well, which was a little confusing.
Did you change at all?
So, it will update, like, if you change at all the boundary on the right.
No, I hadn't… I didn't change anything with the boundary, and I was actually just trying to pull that up and play around with it.
I don't… Mine's not really loading, it says something went wrong, but
Yeah, I don't know, it's something… I did see it, so maybe something to look into.
out here.
Can I share my screen real quick?
Yeah, I'll stop mine.
So, consciousness, this is the, the old version, but if I'm just doing… this quarterly, I'm seeing rent growth of… Minus 36 basis points.
Monthly bumps up to 81 positive.
Yeah, so put your mouse over, like, that rent growth metric.
So this will… so anytime you change it, it's gonna change… percent change in median asking rent since 9-1-2024.
Slip me in.
Gotcha.
Yeah, so that's the.
It would just go based off of…
time frame.
Gotcha, okay.
What the heck did say something went wrong for up there?
What's that about?
Yeah, I don't know that.
That's weird.
Can you hit, can you refresh it real quick?
That shouldn't be, that shouldn't happen.
This one took a while to load, I assume, just because it's a decent number of.
Yeah, it definitely is.
Yeah, the MSA one always… yeah, we're 5,000… almost 6,000 properties, and then we're pulling.
Makes sense.
Yeah, and for forecasting, so right now, I think we have the top 150 MSAs done.
Over the next couple of weeks and months, we'll be taking it actually down to, like, the city and neighborhood level.
So…
There you go.
Okay, that answers that question.
Thanks.
Yeah.
Yeah, anything like that that looks weird, just put your mouse over those headers, and it'll give you, like, the timeframe and everything.
Gotcha.

But yeah, I know that was super quick, but that's kind of the two major updates that we got, so it should be a lot more comparable to CoStar, and that, you know, that market comparison also didn't show, but then you also will have.
The last part of it.

Well then, you also will have this properties, so you actually have the property list for that boundary, and then you can click on into them and get the full, you know, ownership management information, year trends, pricing and availability, estimated rent rolls.
And then we also… this is just another view, like, you could do it this way, too, if you were pulling, like, that specific report.
We'll generate those comps.
Transaction history, demographics, just kind of all in a property view, and then you can just hit generate comms report, and then this will kick you right to that, research report.
Cool.
So, same workflow, but if you kind of wanted to start at, like, a… look at it at, like, a market comparison, and then drill into the actual property itself, you could do that.
Nice.

So is this, that… the new release that you just showed me, is that something you guys are going to demonstrate to the larger group, the next time we all meet, or what's… what's kind of the plan there?
Yeah, the plan is I… I talked with June last week, and he said to kind of give an extra week, just because of, like, with Labor Day, just so everybody was, like, in and out.

I was gonna touch base with him actually tomorrow to see, what time, like, next week worked for the larger group to see everything, because I know forecasting was at the top of the most requested as well, so that's now live.
This is all live.
So yeah, we're excited.

If you… I don't know if you'll see June, you can let them know, but I'm also going to be reaching out to get the… we'll actually activate this for your trial, too, so you'll have the new updated Explore Premium in your trial.
Cool.

I guess the only other last question I have for you guys while you're here is, do you guys have any… I mean, I'm doing my own kind of testing, and I probably will say, like, you know, it's a… it's gonna be a pretty big revenue management focus tool.
June's obviously running the, the kind of selection process here.

Are there any areas that, you know, or things that you would recommend testing that have… you've seen past individuals that have gone with you guys that really liked, or things that, set you apart from competitors that, like, we could specifically test for?
That could make that, kind of, maybe, testing process go a little more in your favor.
Good.
I was gonna say, I think, first of all.
you know, what we have is gonna be the most complete data set.
So, others in the space, they're kind of limited to, like, who they work with, or kind of their data source.
And I'm gonna talk about, like, actually capturing full availability.
So, with us, I mean, if a property's hiding units, or if they are… if it's a lease up, you know, and they don't blast, like, all 300 units on their website, we can still account for them.
So, with that, we're gonna have a heck of a lot better performance metrics.
basically being able to give you… being able to give you a true occupancy, being able to give you accurate leasing, exposure percentages.
I think a big thing, too, is, is leasing velocity.
So, others in the space, I mean, they take a very simple approach that, like, if a unit leaves a website, it's a lease, and that's not necessarily the case.
You have to account for, you know, applications, cancels, denials.
Yeah, of course.

We have a more accurate leasing velocity, I think, too, with historical data, so I think post-pandemic, we're back to some, you know, standardized seasonality now, so having a 5-year look back is going to be helpful.

I also think, too, just with concessions pretty much everywhere across the country right now, we've perfected that concession collection process, so just being able to give you accurate net effective rents, net effective price per feed, all the way down to the unit level is going to be huge.
You guys obviously know what you're doing with the data.
I think we can just give you the best data possible.
Yeah, makes sense.
Shoot, I just forgot a question.

Oh, Do you guys have, like, when you're saying the leasing velocity calculations and all that kind of stuff, is there, like, a definition somewhere of that that says, like, hey, you know, this gets thrown out, or is that something we would get post, you know, signing a contract to see, kind of, inside the black box a little bit?

We can explain it to you, it's probably… it's probably out there somewhere, but just a… to explain it, I think the easiest way to explain it is that if a unit's on a website, it's gonna fall under that available unit bucket.
If tomorrow that unit's gone from the property website, we're gonna assume that somebody put in an application on it?
And then if that unit doesn't reappear on the property website within 7 days, meaning it wasn't canceled or denied, then we move it to a lease.
Gotcha.
Okay.
So what happens…

Frankly, it's a problem I'm trying to tackle myself with, with, like, our demand metric calculations, so, like, you know, days on market type of thing, of trying to understand, hey, if this unit comes back off of a I don't know, 30-day guarantee, which we offer, does that still… do you still start the ticker, or do you remove those 23 days that it was offline, technically, and how do you… I mean, it's… it's just a problem in the industry, I guess.
For sure.
Yeah, absolutely.
Yeah, I think, Who did you guys switch to for rev management?
Are you with Rebo?
with Reba, yeah.
Yeah, I thought so.
Yeah, I don't know, I think,
It seems like the folks that we've been talking to, I mean, it seems like REBA's been pretty well received.
I mean, it seems like everyone's getting away from Ropage, so… I don't know, I'm interested to see how it goes.
I know, I don't know if you guys knew, but we rolled out our own rev management tool, Yeah, oh, about in July we did, yeah.
So, we're kind of going head-to-head with those guys now.
We were a little bit late to the game, I think.

You know, Reba probably beat us by a year or so, but, I don't know, we're having good conversations with it, and I think, like, what makes our tool unique is, we basically have the, the public version of the database that got RealPage in trouble.
Yeah.

Just because we've been tracking this unit-level data every day for the last five years, we have absorption days on market, you know, we track, you know, not just the cheapest one-bedroom in every property, we track literally every one bedroom, every different floor plan.
So, lots of cool information that goes into our model that helps, you know, produce the recommended rents that we're making.
Interesting.
Yeah.
Yeah, Riva's been good so far.
I mean, the key thing is, for us is, you know, it does not take into account any form of… competitor rent in the calculation of, of, Gable's rent.
Yeah, it's interesting.
Whenever we talk to people, whenever we're kind of pitching the rev management tool, I always ask, like, what are your thoughts on competitor data?
And it's always 50-50, it's like, we don't care about it.
I'm biased, I care about it, I think it's important, but yeah, it's… whatever.
I mean, I don't know.

Probably no perfect solution for it, but yeah, I think that's kind of where we're really strong, and then what's great is, I mean, we have, in the system, we have what we call, explainable AI, so every pricing change we make, we actually tell you exactly why the system made those pricing changes.

And what's cool is, like, the breakdown for it is, like, we'll say, hey, we're making these pricing changes because this happened at your property, as well as these factors are what's impacting your comps.
So, just talking with, like, the Arum users, for example, like, it seemed like nobody knew what data was included in that comp set.
You know, you spend 20 minutes trying to figure out why those pricing changes happen, so we try to just make it as easy as possible to understand the recommendations.
If you like them, cool.
If not, you can ignore them.
That's a great idea, well… Yeah, I mean, a lot of the time.
operations would be like, why'd the price change?
They say, well, you know, you're above this threshold, and…
Good.
aggressive factor is set to this, and this time of the year, and it takes time to kind of explain that stuff.
That's… that's a cool feature.
For sure.
But yeah, IQ is the foundation for… we call it Daylight as our rev management tool name, but We can take it through sometime.
Yeah, I'd like that.
But yeah, as far as IQ itself, I know it's kind of an evaluation with a few others, it's definitely gonna just be the historical information we have, the data quality, essentially.
Obviously, it's a data product, I know our data is much more clean and accurate, have a lot of data quality checks in there, like the applications piece.
I don't even think a few of the others even have… they probably just display lease percentage, so that's a big one, too.
Like, we'll have that advertised occupancy.
Yeah, I think, just for your guys' awareness, I think that this new kind of rollout from the market level standpoint is going to be huge for your pitch to us and to the larger group.

You know, a lot of the other tools and a lot of the ways that I know people are using the other tools is not so much of a historical look-back, it's definitely more of a point in time, who's capturing what, where are people's rents at today, what are we missing, or what are we outperforming?

The ability to replace a tool like CoStar with this kind of historical data is, I think, super powerful, and I think what really would set you apart, and so I think that'll be of the utmost importance when presenting to that larger group.
Just being completely frank with you guys, like, a lot of the other tools do a lot of the same stuff for a lot of this kind of, like, day-to-day use, but… Sure.
But I don't think they can replace that coast, and that's been my, kind of.
big takeaway, and… and… is that, look, all these tools are great, but they don't replace what we currently have and what we currently need at a certain level, so…
Yeah, totally.
Yeah, the whole Explore Premium is gonna be a game changer, so… Well, appreciate… appreciate the time, and yeah, we'll see, how the Redskins do tonight.
I don't know with the pack, but it should be a good game.
Yep, yep.
Hopefully, hopefully everyone, I hope it's a good game.
I hope it's something like that, that Ravens, Bills game.
I want, like, high scoring, I want something exciting, I don't want a defensive battle, and I hope Parsons doesn't do his thing at the end of the day.
Yes.

We've dealt with him for too long, I was like, alright, sweet, he's out of the division, and off of my least favorite team in the world, but Sure as shit, we play you guys, we play him two days into the… or 2 weeks into the season.
Yeah, it's funny how it goes, and I just love that the Bears fans will be watching, knowing that they could have had Jaden Daniels.
I think I can't want that.
that.
got a sickly root against Caleb Williams, just, I don't know why.
Even though he's a DC guy, but there's something about him that just, I'm so glad we got Jaden instead.
Yeah, you guys are definitely lucky there, dodged a bullet.
For sure.
Well, cool.
Appreciate it.
out on the golf course, I'm sure, in Scottsdale.
If you're out of here, let us know, for real.
I absolutely will.
I haven't been there in, like… like, 6 years or so, but I'm itching for a golf trip out that way.
What, what logo do you got on there on your quarter zip?
This is, North Barracks, so actually, I lived in Scotland for 3 years, so that was where I was at previous to Gables.
I worked, for a family office that bought a soccer club, kind of did the whole, like, Ted Lasso stuff, so I've played a ton of golf out there.
That's really cool.
Yeah.
Nice.
The Steelers play in Dublin this year, and I got a bunch of buddies going, and a few are doing a golf trip over there, so…
As they showed, the courses are insane over there, too.
Looks awesome, yeah.
Yep, I miss the golf.
That's what I probably need to get out of DC and move.
I set my sights down into, like, South Carolina and some of the courses down there, because I'm… Arizona's a little far from me being on the East Coast, but for sure.
Yeah, I gotta get to better golfing.
Yeah.
Climate, and maybe delete that part of this recording if someone.
Yes.
Yes.
Right?
Alright guys.
Cool.
Thanks, Sam.
Yep, have a good one.
Take care.
